frameworks.name: neurovascular-modulation
frameworks.one_line: direct vascular response to transcranial current as primary tES mechanism

frameworks.name: neurovascular coupling
frameworks.one_line: classical view treating vascular change as secondary to neuronal activity

core_question: Does transcranial electrical stimulation act directly on cerebral vasculature rather than only secondarily through neuronal activity?
key_assumptions: transcranial current density concentrates in cerebrospinal fluid of subarachnoid space
cerebral vasculature lies along dural and pial surfaces where current accumulates
vascular responses to tES are ubiquitous across studies
manipulations: transcranial electrical stimulation applied across cortex
key_findings: evidence reviewed for direct vascular responses to tES independent of neuronal mediation
current concentrates in subarachnoid CSF where vasculature resides
interpretations: direct vascular tES effects may explain part of the variability in tES outcomes
vascular signatures could serve as new biomarkers for stimulation efficacy
